rules.name: RWX Memory Allocation in System Process

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the RWX allocation is a legitimate JIT compiler.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_criteria: If the entity possesses the signal SIG_RWX_INJECTION, the deterministic Sieve engine has already mathematically proven malicious trampoline behavior. The LLM MUST NOT attempt to re-evaluate, classify, or disprove the assembly syntax. Accept the SIG_RWX_INJECTION signal as absolute fact.

false_positive_disproval.description: MsMpEng.exe is the Microsoft Defender engine. It dynamically allocates RWX memory (VadS) for its internal signature scanning and emulation environment. This memory often contains instructions like 'jmp rdx', 'int3', and various register operations. This is completely legitimate behavior for Defender and NOT process injection.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Process is MsMpEng.exe AND malfind telemetry is consistent with emulation (e.g., 'jmp rdx', 'int3') THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: If the process exhibits suspicious memory, you MUST dynamically investigate its origin. Use `request_disk_search` with the process name or 'MEI' to find PyInstaller droppers. If dynamic correlation proves a fileless payload, FAIL to disprove.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF dynamic investigation reveals dropper artifacts THEN FAIL_TO_DISPROVE.

mitre_mapping.procedure: Process Injection: RWX shellcode in system process

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the process name truncation is an artifact of the 15-character EPROCESS limit in the Windows kernel (e.g., smartscreen.ex from smartscreen.exe).
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Process name length is exactly 14 characters plus extension OR is a known truncated baseline process THEN DISPROVE.

rules.name: Staging Archive in Temporary or User Directory

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the archive is a scheduled IT backup OR a benign personal file (like a game, mod, or regular user download) unrelated to any active threat.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF archive name matches corporate backup schema OR file appears to be a legitimate personal download (e.g. video games, mods) without ties to suspicious processes THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the shortcut points to a local directory used for staging confidential corporate intellectual property prior to exfiltration (e.g., copying files from a network share to a local Desktop folder). If the LNK points to confidential project data (like 'Secret Project Data') on a local fixed drive (drive type 3), it is evidence of Data Staging. CRITICAL: The Defense Attorney must NOT overrule a local fixed drive target if the file being staged is highly confidential/leakage material. Do NOT confuse this with USB Exfiltration; this is the precursor Collection phase.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF shortcut clearly points to a benign personal file (e.g. video games, personal docs) or standard Windows component THEN DISPROVE. IF it points to confidential project data on a local drive THEN FAIL_TO_DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the script is part of a legitimate MSI installer or software updater.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Script is known software updater THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the binary belongs to a legitimate self-extracting application or installer.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Binary is a known benign installer THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the secure wipe was performed during authorized decommissioning or normal user cleanup.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF action performed by IT Admin during decommission THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the autorun entry belongs to digitally signed baseline software.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF registry path points to signed standard software THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: CRITICAL: You MUST set request_deep_carve to true to extract strings and verify the presence of USBSTOR keys. If you haven't received carved strings yet, set request_deep_carve=true. If carved strings show USBSTOR or removable media identifiers, FAIL to disprove.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF USB device is corporate issued AND user is authorized THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the file is a sensitive corporate document or intellectual property found on an unauthorized removable media drive. CRITICAL GROUNDING RULE: You MUST cite 'SIG_REMOVABLE_MEDIA_FILE' exactly as a standalone string in your citations. Do NOT combine it with other signals in a single citation string.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Removable media is authorized for this data THEN DISPROVE. IF it is unauthorized data exfiltration THEN FAIL_TO_DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the LNK file points to an explicitly authorized corporate backup network share. The evaluator must strictly verify the destination drive letter and path type. CRITICAL: If the metadata shows a parse error (PATH_BASELINE_PARSE_ERROR) or lnk_drive_type is 0/unknown, you MUST prioritize the 'lnk_carved_strings'. If carved strings show terms like 'Authorized USB' or a volume label indicative of removable media, you MUST assume it is removable media and ignore the broken lnk_drive_type field. Removable media is strictly unauthorized for taking data off-premises. You MUST FAIL TO DISPROVE if the destination appears to be removable media based on carved strings, regardless of parsing errors.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF LNK points to a corporate network share THEN DISPROVE. IF it points to removable media (even via carved strings) THEN FAIL_TO_DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if it is a legitimate IT wiping process.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF IT wiping process THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if LNK is standard user behavior.
